title: Fresh Dense Canadian Fish Soup
description: A hearty, low-calorie Canadian fish soup that showcases poached fillets, fresh shrimp, and a rich mature cheddar swirl for a show‑off finish.
instructions: In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onion, carrot, and celery until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add fish stock, water, bay leaf, dill, and thyme; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat, place cod fillets on top of the broth, cover, and poach for 6–8 minutes or until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Add shrimp and cook for an additional 3 minutes until pink.
Remove fillets and shrimp, shred the fish with a fork, and return to the pot; stir in low‑fat milk, salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Warm the soup to 80°C (176°F) for safe consumption, but do not let it boil.
Pour the soup into bowls, ladle a generous swirl of mature cheddar on top, and sprinkle with extra dill if desired.
Serve immediately with a side of crusty gluten‑free bread if desired.
